chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُحْرِمِ يَحْتَجِمُ أَوْ يَقُصُّ ظُفُراً أَوْ شَعْراً أَوْ شَيْئاً مِنْهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 95 – The one in Ihraam gets cupping done, or he clips nails, or hair, or something from it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/320)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/320)
text: لَا يَحْتَجِمِ الْمُحْرِمُ إِلَّا أَنْ يَخَافَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ أَنْ لَا يَسْتَطِيعَ الصَّلَاةَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Musna Bin Abdul Salaam, from Zurara,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘The one in Ihraam cannot get cupping done unless if he fears upon himself, or unable to Pray’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali from Muthanna’ ibn ‘Abd al-Salam from Zurarah who has said the following: “Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘One in the state of Ihram, must not use cupping (draw blood from one’s body) unless one fears for his life and that one cannot perform Salat (prayer).”’
